items.title: 'A lot of stigma': The 'taboo' cancer side effects we don't talk about

children.content: Shame and stigma mean gynaecological side effects from cancer treatment — affecting women's sexual, urinary and reproductive health — are under-reported and under-researched.

gallery.altText: A composite image of two women side-by-side.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of two women side-by-side.

items.title: China warns Australia over landmark military alliance in the Pacific

children.content: China has warned Australia not to harm its interests in the Pacific after Anthony Albanese struck a landmark military alliance with Fiji.

image.altText: A woman with short black hair stands in front of a podium with the Chinese flag behind her.

items.title: One in every class: AI-assisted sexual abuse widespread among teens

children.content: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

image.altText: Three unidentifiable sets of hands hold mobile phones in a line.

items.title: Hamas to dissolve emergency government in Gaza

children.content: Hamas officials announced the resignation of senior members of the government, but warned that some people would remain in senior positions in Gaza's administration to "prevent any vacuum that could harm our citizens".

image.altText: A column of armed men in military fatigues, black masks and green headbands parade down a street.

items.title: Suburban footballer dies after suffering head injury during game

children.content: Footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald has died after suffering critical head injuries during a reserves game in the city's north on the weekend.

image.altText: Nathan Fitzgerald smiles at the camera with a big thumbs-up

images.altText: Cristiano Ronaldo pours water on his head

articles.title: Trump says he asked FIFA to review US striker's World Cup red card

children.content: Football's European governing body blasts the decision to suspend a US striker's World Cup red-card ban, saying FIFA had "crossed a red line" after US President Donald Trump personally intervened in the case.

image.altText: GettyImages-2250179735

articles.title: England's Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past

children.content: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

image.altText: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham celebrate an England goal

children.content: Portugal takes on Spain in a clash between two Iberian neighbours in Dallas, Texas, after Cristiano Ronaldo confirms this will be his last World Cup. Follow live.

items.title: Ronaldo confirms last World Cup as Portugal meets Spain

updates.title: Ronaldo says this World Cup will be his last

items.title: Dozens killed and more than 100 injured in Sri Lanka prison riot

children.content: Victims with cuts and gunshot injuries have been rushed to hospital near the capital Colombo following the country's deadliest prison riot in years.

image.altText: A dozen prison guards stand around two prison buses.

items.title: 'Disappointing': Prince Harry and King's awkward stand-off over palace

children.content: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.

image.altText: A redheaded man with a beard

items.title: Second Russian attack on Kyiv in less than a week kills 20

children.content: President Volodymyr ⁠Zelenskyy urges NATO to take "strong decisions" on boosting Ukraine's air defences following the strike.

image.altText: Buildings on fire at night as emergency worker tries to douse flames

items.title: When you can claim your phone as a tax deduction and how to do it

children.content: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

image.altText: Close up view of young women using a smartphone on a couch in living room.

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

articles.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

image.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

articles.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?

children.content: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

image.altText: a man with blue aviator sunglasses smiling at the camera with a toddler wearing a black shirt with an Aboriginal flag

articles.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

image.altText: Two men stand with black t-shirts, one man on the left is playing a VR headset.

items.title: NSW government announces review into boy's death amid cannibalism probe

children.content: Cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the NSW Central Coast, with an independent review to take place by the Department of Communities and Justice.

image.altText: Blue teddy bear and other toys on ground

items.title: 'I was feeling threatened': Men removed from Labor conference demand answers

children.content: As Premier Chris Minns walked into the Sydney Town Hall, greeting delegates ahead of his speech, two men unfurled a Palestinian flag from the second-floor balcony before being escorted from the event by police.

image.altText: a man being removed from by police from NSW State Labor Party Conference on Saturda

items.title: How 'exercise snacking' can help you stay on track in winter

children.content: Experts say winter is a good time to double down on exercise rather than taking the foot off the gas.

items.title: The best free TV and movies you can watch now

children.content: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

items.title: 'Pink army' Thunderbirds fans flock to Rundle Mall

children.content: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

image.altText: 'Heil hitler' and a swastika sprayed on a wall of a historic bunker in Albany.

items.title: Ex-university chancellor fronts ICAC days after handing in resignation

children.content: Just days after resigning as chancellor at the University of Wollongong (UOW), Michael Still faces the state's corruption watchdog.

image.altText: Four panel photo with man sitting in a suit as main image

items.title: Hobart deputy lord mayor under fire over taking office snacks

children.content: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

image.altText: A woman and a person whose identity has been obscured outside a doorway.

items.title: AFP confirms threat made against Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi

children.content: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

image.altText: Man wearing suit shakes hands with a second man, standing in front of a wall with two different flags on it.

items.title: Neil the Seal is 1 tonne right now. What happens when he's 4 tonnes?

children.content: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania as well as a growing social media audience. But fears for his wellbeing, and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance, have led to calls to better protect Neil.

gallery.altText: An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

thumbnail.altText: An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.

items.title: Billions in cash can't be accounted for. Where is it all going?

children.content: There is no shortage of theories, from stacks of cash hidden at home to illicit activities, but little in the way of definitive answers.

image.altText: An illustrated closed briefcase with $100 notes sticking out the sides

items.title: Reporting on Venezuela's disaster, we were urgently warned: 'Talk to your embassy'

children.content: Instead of focusing all its efforts on coordinating a widescale rescue operation, the Venezuelan government has been accused of hindering the response, and as has become apparent, our ability to cover it.

image.altText: A man crying while sitting on a wall.

items.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win grand prix after earlier heartbreak

children.content: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.

thumbnail.altText: Four teenage boys holding an Australian flag with a backdrop saying H2 Grand Prix

items.title: More hateful content remains on Meta platforms after changes to moderation

children.content: A Meta executive has downplayed claims a change in the company's policies led to a rise in antisemitism while giving evidence to the royal commission.

items.title: Molineux proves inspired choice after leading Aussies to World Cup win

children.content: Sophie Molineux is praised for her leadership ability after guiding Australia to its Women's T20 World Cup victory.
